data.body: LIMITS + DOGFOOD-CATCH. Joe: 'limits we set during rough tests are MUCH too low' — RAISED max_tokens single-turn/pass1 4000->16000, pass2 per-finding 1500->8000, agentic 1800->8000, aggregate verify 8000->32000; MAX_ITERS 10->20; tool truncation 6000->20000. Committed. EMPIRICAL Q1/Q2 answer (re-ran criteria on the CURRENT 5fd2 — the dogfooding we'd lapsed): E1 at the OLD 4000-cap returned no_tool_use (couldn't complete on the 27KB epic); at 9000 it FAILed MAJOR — the three-pass verdict model is described but had NO covering AC. So the gate DOES catch the description<->AC mismatch (Q2) when (a) re-run after edits and (b) given token budget; we missed it because we never re-ran after ~12 edits + the cap was too low. FIXED: added the three-pass AC to 5fd2; E1 re-run downgraded to MINOR (residual: criteria_v5/v6/v8 version drift in the body). Q1 (children re-decomp not caught): we never ran the gate ON the children + G3/G4 in the harness were fed child TITLES only (not bodies) + they're coverage/consistency not decomposition-FRESHNESS checks + G5 single-turn false-FAILs without children context. The systemic fix = material-edit invalidation FORCING re-review (the gate's own design) — which, un-implemented, we did manually and skipped.
